commentaries.author: Clement Of Alexandria
commentaries.content: Our physical nature rebels against the thought of making the nourishment of the living a garnish for the dead or the cause of life an accessory to the death of the body. .

commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content: 1 Esdras Wisdom of Solomon Wisdom of Sirach Epistle of Jeremiah Prayer of Manasseh The first of the firstfruits of your land you shall bring into the house of the LORD your God. You shall not boil a kid in its mother's milk. The paschal victim must not be so young as to be still suckled. The Samaritan subjoins, "Because that would be like immolating an animal found dead, and the God of Jacob hates it. "(Calmet) Some imagine that this law alludes to a superstitious custom of the pagans, (Spencer, Rit. 8,) or it forbids eating animals while they are, as it were, all milk, not eight days old.
